pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 13 (PROJECT_DIR variable definition), export_result.sh line 8 (PROJECT_DIR usage). The project directory is explicitly created and consistently used throughout task scripts.

setup_task.sh lines 36-44 show the SVG file is synthetically generated (not downloaded) with a specific geometric design containing a triangle (gold #FFC107 and orange #FF5722) and rectangle (#795548) shapes at 512x512 viewBox.

export_result.sh line 47 checks for '263238' in ic_launcher_background.xml. verifier.py lines 87 and 90 expect background color '#263238'. Web search confirms #263238 is Material Design Blue Grey 900 (RGB 38,50,56) - a standard Material Design dark background color used in adaptive icon design.

export_result.sh line 9 defines RES_DIR as '$PROJECT_DIR/app/src/main/res'. Lines 33-40 reference res_dir for checking icon files (mipmap-anydpi-v26/ic_launcher.xml, drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml, values/ic_launcher_background.xml). Android Developer documentation confirms app/src/main/res is the standard resource directory location for Android projects, with mipmap being the correct subdirectory for app launcher icons.

privileged_info_summary: The task generates adaptive icons for a synthetically created Android project (SummitApp) using a procedurally generated SVG logo containing geometric shapes. The background color is specified as #263238 (Material Design Blue Grey 900). All resources are placed in the standard app/src/main/res directory structure. The SVG file is created during setup at /home/ga/Documents/branding/summit_logo.svg and the project directory is /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/SummitApp. All metadata values have been independently verified against task setup scripts, verification logic, and authoritative Android development documentation.
